manifest.title: Norva Email & Resend Audit — 21–22 July 2026
manifest.description: Production-grounded audit of Norva email journeys, delivery reliability, Resend data design and operational controls.

sources.label: Resend Email API — Norva historical messages

query.sql:
with outcomes(outcome, count) as (
  values
    ('Delivered', 116),
    ('Bounced', 10),
    ('Suppressed', 5),
    ('Complained', 1),
    ('Opened', 1),
    ('Clicked', 1)
)
select outcome, count,
       count::numeric / 134 as rate,
       134 as total
from outcomes;
query.description: Reproducible transformation of the reviewed Resend API capture stored at the source path; no recipient-level rows are embedded.
metric_definitions: Outcome rate = outcome count divided by 134 historical Norva messages

sources.label: Norva production Postgres — account and notification snapshot

query.sql:
select
  (select count(*) from auth.users) as auth_users,
  (select count(*) from public.admin_internal_accounts) as internal_accounts,
  (select count(*) from public.cloud_sources) as sources,
  (select count(*) from public.cloud_marketing_email_preferences
   where marketing_email_opt_in is true) as marketing_opt_ins,
  (select count(*) from public.cloud_import_notifications)
    as import_notifications;
query.description: Aggregate production snapshot used to establish population, account class, source readiness, consent and import-email volume.
filters: Aggregate counts only; no direct identifiers exported
metric_definitions: Real customer accounts = auth users not present in admin_internal_accounts
Marketing opt-ins = preferences where marketing_email_opt_in is true

sources.label: Norva repository — email sender and trigger inventory

query.sql:
select *
from (values
  (1, 'Auth actions', 'Transactional auth'),
  (2, 'Security events', 'Transactional security'),
  (3, 'Payment confirmations', 'Transactional billing'),
  (4, 'Catalog import', 'Transactional product'),
  (5, 'AI subtitles', 'Transactional product'),
  (6, 'Support ticket', 'Transactional support'),
  (7, 'Lifecycle', 'Transactional lifecycle'),
  (8, 'Win-back', 'Marketing'),
  (9, 'Operations alerts', 'Operational'),
  (10, 'Account deletion', 'Transactional auth')
) as flows(priority, journey, category);
query.description: Reproducible index of the sender/trigger inventory traced in the source document; delivery and retention annotations are reviewed repository metadata.

sources.label: Resend configuration APIs — Contacts, Segments, Topics, Properties and Webhooks

query.description: Reproducible index of the canonical segment definitions reviewed in the source capture and repository migration.
filters: Projection disabled while Resend team is shared

sources.label: Public DNS snapshot for norva.tv email authentication

cards.description: Messages whose current Resend event is delivered, across the complete historical Norva sample available at audit time.
Historical bounce rate. The ten bounces were generated by deliberately invalid test domains, not customer mailboxes.
Historical complaint rate from one complaint in a tiny, internal-only cohort; unsuitable as a customer benchmark.
Every current Auth account is explicitly classified as an internal, pilot, family or system account.

charts.title: Historical Resend outcomes
charts.subtitle: Current last-event status across 134 Norva messages; exact counts are retained in the source table.
charts.question: How are historical Norva emails distributed across Resend outcomes?
charts.rationale: A bar chart makes the dominant delivered volume and the much smaller failure/engagement outcomes directly comparable.

tables.title: Email journey reliability inventory
tables.subtitle: Every Norva email family, its purpose and the target delivery contract after remediation.

tables.title: Canonical operational segmentation
tables.subtitle: Mutually understandable lifecycle cohorts; contact projection remains gated until Norva owns a dedicated Resend team.

tables.title: Campaign inclusion and exclusion guardrails
tables.subtitle: Orthogonal product-readiness segments never override lifecycle, consent or delivery-safety exclusions.

tables.title: Concrete email template, event and provider matrix
tables.subtitle: Code-traced coverage for every active email family, including all Auth actions, both trial offsets, three dunning stages, billing transitions and support directions.

tables.title: Residual controls and external blockers
tables.subtitle: Items that cannot be truthfully marked complete by application code alone.

blocks.body:
## Executive Summary

Norva’s current email population is entirely internal/test: there are no real customer accounts yet. That makes the historical bounce and complaint percentages useful as engineering signals, but invalid as a customer-quality benchmark. The main problems were architectural: a shared Resend team mixed Norva with BuildTrack, a deprecated all-users audience provided no useful lifecycle targeting, delivery webhooks were absent, and several critical messages could be lost or duplicated around network/database failures.

The remediation replaces direct fire-and-forget paths with immutable, leased outboxes; adds signed, app-scoped delivery telemetry; defines consent-safe lifecycle cohorts; minimizes and expires stored recipient data; and separates a domain-scoped send credential from host-only Resend administration. Contact projection remains intentionally off until Norva is moved to a dedicated Resend team. This is the only safe interpretation of “premium data”: no cross-product contacts, no guessed segments and no false claim that the current five pilot accounts represent customers.

## What the historical delivery data actually says

Delivery dominates the account history, but the failure percentages look severe because the denominator is only 134 messages and the population is deliberately synthetic. All ten bounces came from invalid test domains. One complaint caused subsequent suppressions, including authentication-related traffic, which confirms that complaint handling and application scoping must be observable rather than silently retried. Opens and clicks are not reliable engagement KPIs here: privacy features suppress opens, and most messages were transactional or operational.

## Premium delivery contract by journey

A premium email system acknowledges business state only after the provider accepts the exact immutable request, retries transient/ambiguous outcomes only inside Resend’s idempotency window, quarantines uncertainty instead of risking duplicates, and scrubs personal payloads after completion. Transactional messages ignore marketing opt-out; marketing messages require explicit consent at the final send boundary and carry one-click unsubscribe plus the postal address.

## Segmentation: fewer cohorts, stronger definitions

The legacy “Norva Users” audience contains six contacts, including one stale/deleted address, and cannot answer lifecycle questions. The replacement model uses nine bounded operational Segments, one explicit marketing Topic and typed, minimized Contact Properties. Active and cancellation-scheduled subscriptions are deliberately distinct, as are billing recovery, churn and blocked/suppressed. Signup and activity are exported only as coarse cohorts; exact timestamps and Norva user UUIDs remain inside Norva.

### Campaigns must combine axes explicitly

`catalog-ready` and `onboarding` describe product readiness; they do not grant marketing eligibility and must never erase lifecycle state. Every campaign starts by excluding `internal-pilots` and `blocked-suppressed`, then applies the relevant lifecycle exclusions. Marketing sends additionally require the Topic opt-in, current local consent and no active suppression at the final send boundary. Transactional dunning is not a campaign and remains provider-scoped.

## Provider coverage is not interchangeable

Revolut has Norva-owned payment confirmations, J-3/J-1 trial reminders, three dunning stages, recovery and lifecycle-transition emails. RevenueCat/store rails now feed the same durable confirmation outbox for authoritative paid initial purchases, renewals and non-renewing purchases; trial/intro events remain excluded. A documented RevenueCat `CANCELLATION` with `cancel_reason=CUSTOMER_SUPPORT` now produces one refund confirmation without falsely marking auto-renewal as cancelled, and a later `EXPIRATION` remains the access-revocation signal. Store trial reminders, billing dunning, fiscal receipts and fraud/dispute communications remain provider-owned because Norva must not invent causes or direct store customers to a norva.tv card form.

## Governance, deliverability and data quality

SPF, DKIM and the custom MAIL FROM domain are present. DMARC is missing, so Norva has neither an enforceable anti-spoofing policy nor aggregate reports. Publish a reporting-only DMARC policy first, observe for two to four weeks, then progress to quarantine/reject only when legitimate sources align. Keep operations and customer messaging separate: operational alerts should not inflate lifecycle engagement, and BuildTrack must use another Resend team before Norva Contacts, global unsubscribe, Topics or properties are mutated.

Delivery telemetry contains recipient/from addresses and bounded diagnostic data for incident response. Production scrubs that PII after 90 days, keeps address-free raw event rows for 180 days, and keeps address-free per-message status metrics for 400 days. Active suppression addresses remain only while needed to prevent unsafe delivery; account-deletion confirmation recipients are redacted immediately. False permanent bounces have a service-role-only recovery RPC requiring current confirmed identity, fresh post-bounce mailbox proof and attributable operator evidence. Complaints and provider suppressions remain hard blocks. The append-only recovery audit stores neither address nor raw Auth UUID and expires after 400 days.

## Methodology and interpretation limits

This audit combines a production Postgres snapshot, the complete Resend message history available to the account, live Resend API contract probes, public DNS and repository-level sender/trigger tracing. No recipient address, provider credential, API key, stream credential or payment identifier is included in this artifact.

The historical cohort is five internal/pilot accounts and therefore cannot establish customer engagement, inbox placement or conversion baselines. The report treats delivery events as operational evidence only. The code-level target state is validated by automated tests and production checks; external controls requiring a dedicated Resend team or DNS authority remain explicitly open rather than being simulated.

All current customer-facing email templates are English-only. Template QA in this audit is structural only: multipart presence, escaping, required links, durable delivery contracts and artifact structure were checked, but no cross-client visual certification was completed for Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail, dark/light variants or assistive technologies. This is a documented premium-quality gap, not a claim of email-client parity.

email_flows.delivery: Signed hook; exact payload; stable idempotency key
email_flows.retention: No body ledger; central delivery recipient/diagnostic PII <=90d

email_flows.delivery: Immutable leased outbox; 2xx + provider ID ack
email_flows.retention: Outbox payload scrubbed; central delivery PII <=90d

email_flows.delivery: Ledger-linked outbox; ambiguous quarantine; provider-safe retries (legacy internal flow slug: payment_receipt)
email_flows.retention: Outbox 90d sent / 30d dead-letter; central delivery PII <=90d

email_flows.delivery: Digest outbox; one message per settled user/kind group
Explicit opt-in; leased outbox; current Auth address frozen at send
Durable email outbox plus Telegram channel acknowledgement
Immutable lifecycle outbox; business marker after delivery ack
Final consent gate; one-click unsubscribe; postal footer
email_flows.retention: Consent ledger retained; message PII scrubbed

email_flows.delivery: Explicit mailbox/Telegram only; recovery ack before state deletion
Pre-delete immutable snapshot; post-delete outbox with privacy scrub
email_flows.retention: No stable user identifier after deletion

segments.purpose: Exclude test/family/system accounts from customer KPIs and marketing

segments.definition: Current identity; no blocked/churn/recovery state; setup incomplete
segments.purpose: Help users reach first ready catalog

segments.definition: Cancellation is scheduled at period end while access remains active

segments.purpose: Billing recovery without mixing active/churn cohorts

segments.definition: Entitlement is blocked or delivery suppression applies
At least one ready source; identity neither disabled nor blocked

campaign_guardrails.final_gate: Current local consent; no unsubscribe; no active delivery suppression
Consent for promotional copy; transactional help must be event-triggered
Product-readiness filter is optional context, never eligibility
Consent and suppression rechecked immediately before send
Never include ordinary active-subscriber messaging by overlap

campaign_guardrails.exclude: internal-pilots; blocked-suppressed; trialing; active-subscribers; cancel-scheduled; payment-recovery
campaign_guardrails.final_gate: Explicit consent plus one-click unsubscribe; one bounded send
Catalog readiness cannot override lifecycle, consent or suppression

email_matrix.event: Auth email changed; old address notified
User opt-in plus ready transcript with speech

email_matrix.coverage: Provider-owned; Norva DB reminder excludes these rails
Provider-owned; no incorrect norva.tv card CTA
Covered by the authoritative cross-rail ledger outbox; trial and intro events excluded

email_matrix.event: Successful retry / renewal after past_due or grace

email_matrix.coverage: Covered exactly once; does not imply cancellation or revoke access
Covered but off by default; consent-gated
Covered but off by default; consent-gated

email_matrix.template: store fiscal receipt / fraud or dispute notice
email_matrix.event: Store tax, fraud, chargeback or dispute state
email_matrix.coverage: Provider-owned; Norva sends no accusatory or invented cause

risks.owner_action: Move Norva away from BuildTrack before provisioning Contacts/Segments/Topics
Create dedicated report mailbox/aggregator and publish p=none with strict alignment
Monitor key inventory; Edge has sending-only access and the legacy DB Vault copy is retired
Monitor ingestion lag; a safe Resend delivery probe produced signed sent and delivered events
Use the audited service-role RPC only; complaints and provider suppressions remain hard-blocked
Keep store dunning, fiscal receipts and fraud/dispute notices provider-owned; monitor the RevenueCat webhook contract

risks.state: Client envelope hardened; English-only; structural QA only
risks.owner_action: Define a complete locale policy, then certify representative templates in Gmail, Outlook, Apple Mail, mobile, dark/light and accessibility modes
Start deliverability and lifecycle KPI baselines only after non-internal customers exist
